canonical_definition: In the provided data, Cosine similarity is framed as an objective evaluation procedure used to assess speaker similarity. This method operates by comparing speaker embeddings from synthesized speech against those from reference speech. The single source paper specifies that these embeddings are extracted using models such as ECAPA-TDNN or x-vectors. The sole application described is the quantitative measurement of how similar a synthesized speaker's voice is to a reference voice.
expert_removed_reason: metric/method/tool — not a benchmark or LLM ability
papers: Are Language Models Consequentialist or Deontological Moral Reasoners?
SMEC:Rethinking Matryoshka Representation Learning for Retrieval Embedding Compression
TACO: Enhancing Multimodal In-context Learning via Task Mapping-Guided Sequence Configuration

definitions.text: An objective evaluation procedure that compares speaker embeddings from synthesized and reference speech to assess speaker similarity.
definitions.paper: TACO: Enhancing Multimodal In-context Learning via Task Mapping-Guided Sequence Configuration
